body_text:
"Yama-budō (Vitis coignetiae): fruit eaten raw and used for wine; leaves substituted for tobacco.
"Ebi-dzuru (V. thunbergii): fruit eaten raw, leaves cleaned and cooked; worm inside the cane baked and eaten by children as remedy for convulsions.
"Sankaku-dzuru (V. flexuosa): fruit eaten raw.
"Ama-dzuru (V. saccharifera): fruit eaten raw; children are very fond of eating the leaves, as they contain sugar."
